story_result.smoke_test: HTTP 307 → /login on /setup (auth proxy firing in production)

implemented.details: Template picker UI on /setup loads the 3 seeded residential templates (Standard Timber Frame, Slab-on-Grade Single Storey, ICF Two-Storey) and summarizes each server-side via summarizeTemplate (AC-TB-1). Click-to-select highlights the active card and drives the preview panel showing stages with colored left-border, sample tasks per stage (capped at 3, '+N more' tail), and the template's total duration, stage count, task count (AC-TB-2). 'Use this template' triggers the applyTemplate server action which copies stages, tasks, and materials into the user's project with sequential date scheduling (AC-TB-3): buildTemplateInsertPlan walks a cursor day-by-day starting at project.start_date, planned_end = planned_start + duration_days, next task planned_start = prev planned_end + 1. order_by_date = planned_start - lead_time_days for each material. Server-side summarisation keeps the browser payload small and render under 2s (AC-TB-4). After apply, /setup detects existing stages for the project and shows a 'Template applied' holding view (customization story will refine). 12 new unit tests covering insert plan computation + template summary; all 43 suite tests pass; build + typecheck clean; production deploy smoke-tested (307 → /login on /setup, auth proxy firing).

ProjectCreationForm client component with React 19 useTransition for pending state. Pure validation helpers in lib/projects/validate.ts (name required, start_date required + past-date warning with explicit checkbox confirmation, valid date parsing). localStorage draft persistence under versioned key 'cm:project-creation-draft:v1' — restores name/address/start_date on remount, cleared after successful submit. Server action app/actions/projects.ts re-runs validation server-side, inserts Project with user_id from authenticated session and status='setup', revalidates /, redirects to /setup. /setup page routes: no project → creation form; status='setup' → template browser placeholder (next story); status='active' → /briefing. Root / route already handled AC-PS-4 (redirect based on project presence/status) from foundation. Warm earth-tone design tokens applied (clay primary, amber warning, sage accents). 13 unit tests covering all 5 ACs. Total suite: 34 tests passing.

12 tables: projects, stages, trades, tasks, task_dependencies, materials, photos, documents, push_subscriptions, notification_preferences, shift_alerts, templates. RLS on all tables. cascade_task_dates() PostgreSQL function (recursive CTE). 14 performance indexes including partial index on materials.order_by_date WHERE order_status = 'not_ordered'. set_updated_at() trigger on all mutable tables. Storage buckets: photos (10MB, image/*) and documents (25MB, pdf/docx/xlsx). 4 migrations pushed to joxusnbbsoqkoocwztmz (eu-west-1). Templates seeded with 3 Irish residential builds (Standard Timber Frame 365d, Slab-on-Grade 280d, ICF Two-Storey 390d). Seed data: O'Brien Timber Frame House, 7 stages, 22 tasks, 10 materials, 3 trades.

Server client (createClient via @supabase/ssr + cookies()), browser singleton client, proxy.ts for Next.js 16 session refresh middleware (replaces middleware.ts). Login/signup/forgot-password pages with warm earth-tone styling. Server action for signout. Root redirect: unauthenticated→/login, no project→/setup, active project→/briefing.

photos bucket (10MB, jpeg/png/webp/heic, private) and documents bucket (25MB, pdf/docx/xlsx/images, private). RLS policies: authenticated users can INSERT/SELECT/DELETE. Signed URL pattern for downloads (60s expiry). setup-buckets.ts helper for programmatic bucket creation if needed.

Custom service worker (not next-pwa — removed as webpack-only incompatible with Next.js 16 Turbopack). Network-first with cache fallback for app routes. Cache-first for /_next/static/ immutable assets. Supabase API: network-only. Offline shell caches /briefing and /materials. Push + notificationclick handlers. Web App Manifest with shortcuts (Briefing, Schedule). sw.js served with no-cache header via vercel.json. ServiceWorkerRegister client component for registration.

VAPID keys generated and stored in .env.local + Vercel env vars. POST /api/push/subscribe saves subscription to push_subscriptions. DELETE unsubscribes. GET /api/cron/daily-check: Vercel Cron (7am daily), sends push for overdue tasks and upcoming order deadlines, respects notification_preferences. Cron protected by CRON_SECRET. webpush.setVapidDetails moved inside handler (not module level) to avoid Turbopack module evaluation error.

Warm earth-tone design tokens: --color-primary #8B5E3C (clay), --color-secondary #87A96B (sage), --color-accent #D4A355 (amber), --color-bg #FAF7F2 (warm off-white). AppShell: fixed sidebar (240px, desktop ≥768px) with primary + secondary nav, sign-out form action. Mobile: fixed bottom tab bar (56px, 4 primary routes). Placeholder pages for all routes with skeleton loading pattern. EmptyState, Skeleton, CardSkeleton, ErrorBoundary components. lucide-react icons throughout.

Vercel project prj_S1ciXeW4V0GvMCBkjpRyUeZ6KW6Y (mtgordle). All 7 env vars set in Vercel dashboard (NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, NEXT_PUBLIC_VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY, VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY, VAPID_SUBJECT, CRON_SECRET). Deployed to production: https://mtgordle.vercel.app. Hobby tier: production deploys only (--prod flag).

Realistic Irish construction seed: O'Brien Timber Frame House, 42 Laragh Lane, Wicklow. 7 stages (Foundation→Finishes), 22 tasks with realistic dependencies (excavation→footings→slab→frame→roof→etc.), 10 materials with lead times (Ready Mix Concrete 3d, Timber Frame Kit 70d, Windows 42d, MVHR 28d, Slates 21d). 3 trades: structural engineer, groundworks contractor, timber frame supplier.

divergences.actually_did: Custom hand-written service worker at public/sw.js
divergences.rationale: next-pwa v5 is webpack-only and incompatible with Next.js 16 Turbopack (default bundler). Custom service worker is actually simpler, has no Workbox dependency, and is fully portable. Push and notification handlers work identically.

divergences.actually_did: proxy.ts with proxy() function and proxyConfig export
divergences.rationale: Next.js 16 renamed middleware.ts to proxy.ts. Confirmed by Vercel platform context. The ƒ Proxy (Middleware) entry in the build output confirms it is correctly detected.

factory_decisions.decision: Sequential date scheduling (no dependency graph) for template-apply
factory_decisions.context: Templates carry stages/tasks/materials but no task-dependency info. Template-apply needs to satisfy tasks.planned_start NOT NULL + planned_end NOT NULL on insert. Options: leave dates null (rejected by schema), chain linearly, or attempt to infer dependencies from task order.
alternatives_considered: Defer date computation to a separate cascade step (breaks schema NOT NULL constraints; would need migration)
Template carries explicit depends_on — requires reshaping seed JSONB and template-customization story handles dependency editing already
All tasks share project.start_date — loses the ability to show a meaningful schedule in the Gantt before customization
factory_decisions.rationale: Cursor-based linear schedule: planned_start of task N = planned_end of task N-1 + 1 day, planned_end = planned_start + duration_days. Produces a reasonable initial Gantt immediately after template apply; the user edits durations and dependencies in the template-customization + task-management stories and cascade_task_dates() refines from there. Pure function keeps the scheduling logic unit-testable without DB round-trips.

factory_decisions.decision: Server-side template summarization (summarizeTemplate) — send TemplateSummary to the browser, not the full JSONB
factory_decisions.context: Each template stores a fat stages JSONB (whole build with materials). Sending all 3 to the client for list + preview would waste ~15KB and block the 2s render target in AC-TB-4.
alternatives_considered: Ship raw templates array, summarize in the client — duplicates logic + bloats JS bundle
Paginate / lazy-load preview — overkill for 3 seeded templates
Render preview server-side with server actions — adds round-trip on every card click
factory_decisions.rationale: /setup computes TemplateSummary (stage/task counts, sample_tasks per stage, total_duration_days with computed fallback) in the RSC, passes only summaries to the TemplateBrowser client component. Apply still reads the full JSONB server-side via templateId. Keeps client payload small, server logic centralized, and preview switching instant.

factory_decisions.decision: Detect already-applied-template by counting stages on the project rather than a project flag
factory_decisions.context: After apply, the project is still status='setup' (customization story flips to 'active'). Navigating back to /setup needs to avoid letting the user apply a second template on top of the first.
alternatives_considered: Add a projects.template_id or projects.template_applied_at column — schema churn for a UI guard
Redirect to a dedicated /setup/customize route after apply — premature; customization story hasn't defined that route yet
Client-side localStorage flag — unreliable across devices
factory_decisions.rationale: A quick HEAD count on stages WHERE project_id = X is cheap (partial index exists), stateless, and inherently correct: if any stages exist, the template was applied. Works even if the user opened /setup on a second device. Zero schema changes.

factory_decisions.decision: Use local synthetic ids ('s0', 't0-1') in the insert plan to join inserts across three tables
factory_decisions.context: applyTemplate has to insert stages, then tasks (needing the stage's real id), then materials (needing the task's real id). supabase.insert().select() returns real ids but not in the same order as input, so a join key is required.
alternatives_considered: Insert stages one at a time to capture each id inline — N round trips instead of 1
Rely on array index ordering — Supabase does not guarantee it
Use Postgres CTE with WITH clauses — requires a stored procedure or rpc, more infra than needed
factory_decisions.rationale: Insert plan tags each row with a deterministic _localId ('s{order_index}', 't{stageIdx}-{taskIdx}'), and both stages.order_index and tasks.(stage_id + order_index) are carried through inserts. The server action rebuilds the local→real mapping from the returned rows after each insert. Three round trips total, correct by construction.

factory_decisions.context: AC-PS-1/2/3 involve branching validation (empty name, past date warning, confirmation flag). The form needs client-side validation for UX; the server action needs identical validation for safety. Two code paths = duplication risk.
alternatives_considered: Validate only server-side (requires round-trip for every validation error — poor mobile UX)
Validate only client-side (security hole — malicious clients can POST invalid data)
Schema library (zod) — another dep for 5 rules this simple
factory_decisions.rationale: Pure functions accepting (values, today) return { errors, warnings }. Used by both the form and the server action. 13 unit tests run in <10ms, no mocks needed. McKinley: boring technology — plain TypeScript, no runtime deps.

factory_decisions.decision: Past-date confirmation via checkbox inside an amber warning panel (not browser confirm())
factory_decisions.context: AC-PS-3 requires 'explicit confirmation' for past start_date. Options: native window.confirm(), inline checkbox, two-step submit.
alternatives_considered: window.confirm() — blocking modal, inaccessible, ugly on mobile
Two-step submit (first click shows warning, second submits) — confusing UX, easy to double-click through
Modal dialog — overkill for a single confirmation
factory_decisions.rationale: Inline amber panel with a checkbox: visible, accessible, preserves form context, prevents accidental submits. Confirmation state resets whenever the date changes so the user re-confirms if they adjust.

factory_decisions.decision: localStorage draft persistence stored under versioned key 'cm:project-creation-draft:v1'
factory_decisions.context: AC-PS-5: form restore across sessions. Need a key that won't collide with other drafts and can be migrated.
alternatives_considered: sessionStorage (lost on tab close, fails AC-PS-5)
IndexedDB (overkill for 3 string fields)
Unversioned key 'project-draft' (can't migrate schema without orphaning old drafts)
factory_decisions.rationale: Namespaced + versioned key allows future form schema changes. Draft is cleared after successful createProject redirect (returns to same page, effect unmounts, clearDraft called in the success path).

factory_decisions.decision: Cast-at-site for projects.insert() rather than editing lib/supabase/types.ts
factory_decisions.context: Foundation eval flagged lib/supabase/types.ts as lacking Relationships[] on every table, which causes typed Postgrest inserts to resolve to 'never'. Options: regenerate types, add Relationships: [] to all 12 tables, or cast at call sites.
alternatives_considered: Edit all 12 tables to add Relationships: [] — touches 12 tables for a single insert, high blast radius
Regenerate types via supabase gen types — correct long-term fix but requires Supabase CLI auth and migration roundtrip, out of scope for this story
Disable strict typing globally — defeats the purpose
factory_decisions.rationale: Single targeted cast in app/actions/projects.ts with a comment pointing at the foundation-eval finding. The typed Row/Insert shapes are still used by app/page.tsx and app/(app)/setup/page.tsx for reads. A dedicated types-regeneration pass belongs in a separate chore, not buried inside a feature story.

factory_decisions.decision: Removed next-pwa, wrote custom service worker
factory_decisions.context: next-pwa v5 injects webpack config, incompatible with Next.js 16 Turbopack default. Build failed with 'This build is using Turbopack, with a webpack config and no turbopack config.'
alternatives_considered: @ducanh2912/next-pwa — Turbopack-compatible fork, but adds another dependency for a problem solvable with 80 lines of vanilla JS
next build --webpack — disables Turbopack, slower builds, not idiomatic for Next.js 16
factory_decisions.rationale: A custom sw.js at public/sw.js served with no-cache headers is the recommended Next.js 16 approach. No Workbox dependency, full control over caching strategy, easier to debug.

factory_decisions.context: Git branch build/mtgordle matched both the Supabase project name and Vercel project name. ACTIVE_HEALTHY status confirmed.
alternatives_considered: Create new Supabase project — would require pausing an existing project (at 2/2 free tier limit) and waiting for activation
Use 'openfleet' project — wrong project, different product
factory_decisions.rationale: The naming alignment (build/mtgordle branch + mtgordle Supabase + mtgordle Vercel project) is unambiguous. Project was pre-created as part of the build setup.

factory_decisions.context: Calling setVapidDetails at module evaluation time caused build failure on Vercel: 'No key set in vapidDetails.privateKey'. Turbopack evaluates route modules during static page generation.
alternatives_considered: Lazy initialization singleton — adds unnecessary complexity
Dynamic import of web-push — works but slower
factory_decisions.rationale: Moving setVapidDetails inside the handler is the simplest fix. It runs once per cold start in production (Fluid Compute reuses instances). No performance impact.

factory_decisions.decision: cascade_task_dates() implemented as PostgreSQL function with recursive CTE
factory_decisions.context: Cascade engine spec requires single-transaction update of all downstream tasks. Acceptable: under 2s for 500 tasks.
alternatives_considered: Application-layer BFS in TypeScript — multiple round trips, not atomic, slower for large graphs
PostgreSQL stored procedure with LOOP — not set-based, harder to optimize
factory_decisions.rationale: Recursive CTE traverses the full DAG in one query, updates all affected tasks + materials atomically. PostgreSQL can optimize the traversal with the existing task_dependencies indexes.

factory_decisions.decision: shift_alerts table for briefing delta tracking
factory_decisions.context: Daily briefing needs to show 'what changed since last visit'. Options: compute on-the-fly (expensive join), event log (append-only), or client-side localStorage.
alternatives_considered: On-the-fly computation — requires tracking last_visit_at per user and joining across all cascades
localStorage — lost on device switch, not reliable for mobile
Supabase Realtime subscription — overkill for a daily summary
factory_decisions.rationale: Server-side event log (shift_alerts) written by the cascade function. Dismissed flag per user. Readable from any device. Foundation builds the table; daily-experience feature cycle builds the UI.

factory_questions.question: Supabase Auth email confirmation: Vercel production URL must be added to Supabase allowed redirect URLs before signup emails will work.

cascade_task_dates() does not currently write to shift_alerts. The cascade function updates task dates and material order_by_dates but does not log the changes to shift_alerts. This must be added when implementing the daily-briefing feature area.
factory_questions.resolved_as: Deferred to daily-briefing feature cycle (shift-alerts story)

notes: cascade_task_dates() SQL function is written but feature build must wire it to task update API routes via supabase.rpc('cascade_task_dates', {p_task_id, p_new_planned_end})
cascade_task_dates() does NOT yet write to shift_alerts — add that in the shift-alerts story
AI assist (template-customization story) requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY — deferred to feature build
Supabase Auth email confirmation requires production URL in Supabase Redirect URLs config before signup emails work
Seed data requires real auth.users entry — run locally with supabase db seed after creating dev user

findings: All 12 tables present: projects, stages, trades, tasks, task_dependencies, materials, photos, documents, push_subscriptions, notification_preferences, shift_alerts, templates. All feature areas can build without ALTER TABLE.
cascade_task_dates() PostgreSQL function (recursive CTE) covers scheduling-engine requirements atomically.
RLS enabled and policies configured on all 12 tables.
14 performance indexes including the critical partial index on materials.order_by_date WHERE order_status='not_ordered'.

FINDING (non-blocking): lib/supabase/types.ts was not regenerated after the templates migration (20260405194448_add_templates_table.sql). The templates table is absent from the Database type. Feature builders querying templates via the typed Supabase client will need 'as any' casts. types/database.ts is authoritative and correct.

FINDING (non-blocking): lib/supabase/types.ts has materials.quantity typed as 'number | null' but the SQL schema defines it as TEXT. types/database.ts correctly types it as 'string | null'. Feature code should import from types/database.ts.
FINDING (non-blocking): cascade_task_dates() does not yet write to shift_alerts — explicitly deferred to daily-briefing feature cycle and documented in factory_questions.
lib/supabase/server.ts: createClient() uses @supabase/ssr with cookie-aware session handling. setAll() swallows errors in Server Component context (correct pattern).
lib/supabase/client.ts: Browser singleton pattern, correct for client-side use.
lib/supabase/service.ts: createServiceClient() uses SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY with autoRefreshToken:false. Clear warning comment present.
proxy.ts: Session refresh via getUser() on every request. Auth route protection and static asset bypass configured correctly. proxyConfig matcher excludes api/push and api/cron.

web-push: webpush.setVapidDetails() called inside handler (not at module level) — correct fix for Turbopack module evaluation issue. CRON_SECRET protection on cron and send routes. 410 (expired subscription) handling present.

FINDING (non-blocking): Cron handler uses INNER JOIN for notification_preferences (.select('*, notification_preferences!inner(*)')). Users with push subscriptions but no notification_preferences row will be silently excluded from notifications. Feature build should ensure notification_preferences is created on signup.

FINDING (non-blocking): Storage RLS policies check auth.uid() IS NOT NULL only — not project ownership. Any authenticated user with a direct storage path can read/delete another user's files. The signed URL pattern (60s expiry) and application-layer path construction provide practical protection, but this is weaker than the table-level RLS which joins through projects.

All env vars referenced by name (NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, NEXT_PUBLIC_VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY, VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY, VAPID_SUBJECT, CRON_SECRET). No hardcoded values found.
Login: app/(auth)/login/page.tsx — signInWithPassword with error handling and loading state. Redirects to /briefing on success.
Signup: app/(auth)/signup/page.tsx — present per files_changed.
Forgot password: app/(auth)/forgot-password/page.tsx — present.
Logout: signOut server action in app/actions/auth.ts — supabase.auth.signOut() + redirect('/login').
Session persistence: proxy.ts calls supabase.auth.getUser() on every request, forwarding all cookies via supabaseResponse. Correct @supabase/ssr pattern.
Protected routes: proxy.ts redirects unauthenticated users → /login for all non-static, non-api paths.
Auth callback: app/auth/callback/route.ts — handles email confirmation code exchange.

FINDING (configuration, non-blocking): Supabase Auth email confirmation requires the production URL (https://mtgordle.vercel.app) to be added to Supabase's Site URL and Redirect URLs allowlist before signup confirmation emails will complete. This is a manual configuration step, not a code failure. Flagged as 'significant' in factory_questions.
AppShell: Fixed 240px sidebar (desktop ≥768px) with primary nav (Briefing, Schedule, Materials, Trades) and secondary nav (Stages, Photos, Settings). Mobile bottom tab bar (4 primary routes, 56px).
Design tokens applied consistently: --color-primary #8B5E3C (clay), --color-secondary #87A96B (sage), --color-accent #D4A355 (amber), --color-bg #FAF7F2.
ErrorBoundary: Proper React class component with getDerivedStateFromError and 'Try again' recovery action.
Loading states: Skeleton, CardSkeleton, EmptyState components present.
Placeholder pages for all feature routes (briefing, schedule, materials, trades, stages, photos, settings, setup, stages/[id], tasks/[id]).
Navigation covers all feature areas accessible to the owner-builder. No document-specific top-level route — documents are accessed via task/stage detail pages per the vision scope.
Production deployment confirmed: https://mtgordle.vercel.app returns HTTP 307 → /login (auth middleware firing correctly in production).

All 7 env vars set in Vercel dashboard: NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, NEXT_PUBLIC_VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY, VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY, VAPID_SUBJECT, CRON_SECRET.
vercel.json: sw.js served with no-cache header (Cache-Control: no-store, must-revalidate). Cron job at /api/cron/daily-check scheduled 0 7 * * * (7am daily).
Hobby tier limitation documented: production-only deploys, --prod flag required.
21 tests passing across 2 test files (2 test files, schema.test.ts + auth.test.ts). All 21 pass.
Seed data: O'Brien Timber Frame House, 42 Laragh Lane, Wicklow — realistic Irish address, project starting 2026-06-01.
7 stages matching a genuine Irish residential build sequence (Foundation, Frame, Roof, External Envelope, Internal Works, Services, Finishes).
22 tasks with authentic Irish construction terminology (ETICS, MVHR, PIR, Blower door test, Bangor Blue slate, Zehnder ComfoAir).
10 materials with realistic Irish suppliers (Roadstone Concrete Bray, Ennio Steel Dublin, Wicklow Timber Systems, Xtratherm, Welsh Slate Ltd, Zehnder Ireland, Internorm via Passive House Systems).
Lead times domain-appropriate: 3d concrete, 5d mesh, 70d timber frame kit, 42d windows, 21d slate, 28d MVHR.
Task dependency chain is a valid, realistic DAG covering the full build sequence.
3 residential templates seeded (Standard Timber Frame 365d, Slab-on-Grade 280d, ICF Two-Storey 390d) with nested JSONB stage/task/material definitions.
Notification preferences seeded for test user.

evidence: next-pwa replaced with custom service worker: fully documented divergence with technical justification (Turbopack incompatibility). Not degradation — simpler and more maintainable.
middleware.ts renamed to proxy.ts: Next.js 16 platform requirement, documented. Not degradation.

templates.stages uses JSONB: appropriate design for a template catalog (exploded into rows at project creation). Not a case of avoidance — the actual data tables (tasks, materials, stages) all use typed columns.
No TODO comments found in integration wrappers.
No always-passing test stubs found. Auth tests use explicit mocks with correct @supabase/ssr mock structure.

FINDING (quality issue, not silent degradation): lib/supabase/types.ts has schema drift — missing templates table, materials.quantity typed as number|null (should be string|null), tasks.planned_start/planned_end typed as nullable (should be non-null). The database type file was not regenerated after the templates migration. Feature builders should use types/database.ts for domain types. The typed Supabase client will require 'as any' for templates queries.

structural_gaps: lib/supabase/types.ts missing templates table — will force 'as any' casts in feature code querying templates
lib/supabase/types.ts has materials.quantity: number|null vs TEXT in SQL — incorrect type inference for Supabase client queries
cascade_task_dates() does not write to shift_alerts — known gap, deferred to daily-briefing cycle
Supabase Auth redirect URL configuration outstanding — signup email confirmation won't complete without manual config in Supabase dashboard
integration_gaps: Storage RLS allows any authenticated user to read/delete any storage object by path — weaker than table-level RLS. Acceptable for foundation; tighten in photo-storage feature cycle if needed.
Cron INNER JOIN on notification_preferences will silently skip users without a preferences record — daily-briefing feature cycle must ensure preferences are created on user signup.
recommendations: Update lib/supabase/types.ts to include the templates table and fix materials.quantity type before the project-setup feature cycle begins. Run: npx supabase gen types typescript or manually add templates to the Database interface.
Add Vercel production URL to Supabase Auth redirect URLs before testing end-to-end signup flow.
In the daily-briefing cycle, ensure notification_preferences row is inserted on first auth (upsert in the auth callback or signup action).
In the photo-storage cycle, consider tightening storage RLS to restrict by path prefix (user_id) rather than just auth.uid() IS NOT NULL.
